options.label: Automations that map IDE events to agent actions
Markdown guides that direct the agent's behavior in every interaction
Reusable instructions activated on demand to load specific context
Connections to external tools and services through a standardized protocol

questions.explanation: Hooks in Kiro are automations that connect IDE events (like file editing, prompt submission) to agent actions (askAgent or runCommand). When the event occurs, the specified action is executed automatically.

questions.text: A Hook in Kiro can be configured to automatically run a lint command whenever a TypeScript file is saved.
questions.explanation: Hooks support the `fileEdited` event (displayed as 'File Save' in the UI) with file pattern filtering (filePatterns). Combined with the 'Shell Command' action (runCommand), it's possible to automatically run lint when saving .ts files.

questions.text: You want Kiro to automatically review every file write operation before executing it. Which Hook configuration is appropriate?

options.label: eventType: preToolUse, hookAction: askAgent, toolTypes: write
eventType: fileEdited, hookAction: runCommand, command: git diff
eventType: postToolUse, hookAction: runCommand, command: npm test

questions.explanation: The 'preToolUse' event with toolTypes 'write' intercepts write operations before execution. Combined with 'askAgent', Kiro can review and validate the operation before proceeding.

questions.text: Arrange the Hook event types from earliest to latest in the execution cycle:

options.label: Prompt Submit - when the user sends a prompt
Pre Tool Use - before the agent uses a tool
Post Tool Use - after the agent uses a tool
Agent Stop - when the agent finishes its response turn

questions.explanation: The IDE hook execution cycle follows: the user submits a prompt (Prompt Submit), the agent invokes tools (Pre Tool Use / Post Tool Use intercept before and after each invocation), and finally the agent completes its turn (Agent Stop). File Save (fileEdited) is a separate event triggered when files are saved.
